books.title: YESTERYEAR
books.author: Caro Claire Burke
books.description: Natalie Heller Mills, a privileged tradwife social media influencer, wakes up to find she is living in the comparatively difficult reality of 1855.

books.title: WHISTLER
books.author: Ann Patchett
books.description: Decades after their time together, a woman reconnects with her stepfather and they look back at the directions their lives have taken.

books.title: THE CALAMITY CLUB
books.author: Kathryn Stockett
books.description: As things get tougher during the Depression, several women look for ways to improve their circumstances.

books.title: THE EXQUISITE TORMENT OF LOVING YOUR ENEMY
books.author: Brigitte Knightley
books.description: The second book in the Dearly Beloathed series. Boundaries weaken between an assassin and a healer.

books.title: LOVE YOU MORE
books.author: Emily Giffin
books.description: Shortly after becoming engaged, Billie receives a call from her first love that compels her to return to Wisconsin.

books.title: THE CORRESPONDENT
books.author: Virginia Evans
books.description: Letters from someone she used to know push Sybil Van Antwerp toward revisiting her past and finding a way to forgive.

books.title: CARL'S DOOMSDAY SCENARIO
books.author: Matt Dinniman
books.description: The second book in the "Dungeon Crawler Carl" series. Carl and Donut confront an ancient spell and heightened dangers.

books.title: THE SHAMPOO EFFECT
books.author: Jenny Jackson
books.description: When Caroline Lash is pushed out of a social circle in a small seaside town, she exposes secrets that rile up the residents.

books.title: THE DUNGEON ANARCHIST'S COOKBOOK
books.author: Matt Dinniman
books.description: The third book in the "Dungeon Crawler Carl" series. Carl and Donut are up against a subway system filled with monsters.

books.title: DUNGEON CRAWLER CARL
books.author: Matt Dinniman
books.description: A Coast Guard vet named Carl and his ex-girlfriend’s cat, Princess Donut, are trapped in a fantasy dungeon.

books.title: COUNTRY PEOPLE
books.author: Daniel Mason
books.description: When his wife accepts a visiting professorship in Vermont, Miles encounters a story that may have more to it than seems at first glance.

books.title: HE WHO FIGHTS WITH MONSTERS, BOOK 1
books.author: Travis Deverell
books.description: A middle manager at an office supplies store becomes an interdimensional adventurer.

books.title: THE BUTCHER'S MASQUERADE
books.author: Matt Dinniman
books.description: The fifth book in the "Dungeon Crawler Carl" series. The action moves to the sixth floor, which is known as the Hunting Grounds.

books.title: THE GATE OF THE FERAL GODS
books.author: Matt Dinniman
books.description: The fourth book in the "Dungeon Crawler Carl" series. Obstacles abound on the fifth floor of the dungeon.

books.title: EVERYTHING WAS BEAUTIFUL AND NOTHING HURT
books.author: Ben Reeves
books.description: A man who gives comfort to those who are dying bonds with a midwife and her daughter.

books.title: REGIME CHANGE
books.author: Maggie Haberman and Jonathan Swan
books.description: Two White House correspondents for The New York Times delve into the first year of President Trump's second term.

books.title: REVOLUTION
books.author: Eric Metaxas
books.description: The author of “Martin Luther” and “Bonhoeffer” gives an account of the foundation of the United States of America.

books.title: CANCEL ME IF YOU CAN
books.author: Dave Portnoy
books.description: The founder of Barstool Sports describes how he grew his business from a four-page newspaper into the digital media company it is today.

books.title: STRANGERS
books.author: Belle Burden
books.description: Burden retraces her marriage of 20 years in search of clues to help shape her understanding about its demise and to find a way forward.

books.title: COMMUNION
books.author: JD Vance
books.description: The vice president and author of “Hillbilly Elegy” describes how he perceives his faith played a part in his work in public life.

books.title: THE LAND AND ITS PEOPLE
books.author: David Sedaris
books.description: Essays on the passage of time, complicated relationships and some unexpected delights life has to offer.

books.title: LONDON FALLING
books.author: Patrick Radden Keefe
books.description: The author of “Say Nothing” details the efforts by the parents of a 19-year-old Londoner to uncover the truth about his mysterious death and secret life.

books.title: FAMESICK
books.author: Lena Dunham
books.description: The author of “Not That Kind of Girl” evaluates the effects that pursuing her creative endeavors had on her.

books.title: THE ANXIOUS GENERATION
books.author: Jonathan Haidt
books.description: A co-author of “The Coddling of the American Mind” looks at the mental health impacts that a phone-based life has on children.

books.title: EVERYTHING IS TUBERCULOSIS
books.author: John Green
books.description: The author of “The Anthropocene Reviewed” chronicles the fight against the deadly infectious disease tuberculosis.

books.title: AMERICA, U.S.A.
books.author: Eddie S. Glaude Jr
books.description: The author of “Begin Again” delves into elements of America’s difficult and complicated past, and what they portend for our future.

books.title: ON COURAGE
books.author: Julia Angwin and Ami Fields-Meyer
books.description: The authors of the New Yorker essay “So You Want to Be a Dissident?” profile people who stood up to authoritarianism.

books.title: THE GREATEST SENTENCE EVER WRITTEN
books.author: Walter Isaacson
books.description: The historian and biographer examines the concepts of a statement found in the Declaration of Independence.

books.title: WHEN BREATH BECOMES AIR
books.author: Paul Kalanithi
books.description: A memoir by a physician who received a diagnosis of Stage IV lung cancer at the age of 36.

books.title: SUICIDAL EMPATHY
books.author: Gad Saad
books.description: The host of the podcast “The Saad Truth” argues that what he considers misguided compassion is having a negative impact on society.
